new_findings.title: Failure to update Oracle due to rounding issue leading incorrectness of the orderbook
new_findings.reason: Rounding issues could prevent oracle updates in PriceManager despite SafeCast protections.

new_findings.title: Incorrect Oracle Selection in Solvency Validation
new_findings.reason: Dual oracle system could have incorrect oracle selection logic for different validation contexts.

new_findings.title: Missing check if Chainlink sequencer is down
new_findings.reason: PriceManager.sol dual oracle may lack L2 sequencer uptime validation if deployed on Arbitrum/Optimism.

new_findings.title: Using the same heartbeat for all Chainlink feeds will either result in frequent reverts or stale prices
new_findings.reason: Using uniform heartbeat across different Chainlink feeds could cause frequent reverts or stale prices.

new_findings.title: Use of deprecated oracle interface function for fetching asset prices
new_findings.reason: If PriceManager._getAssetPrice() uses deprecated latestAnswer() instead of latestRoundData().

new_findings.title: `latestAnswer()` from Chainlink is deprecated and can return inaccurate price
new_findings.reason: Same deprecated API usage risk as finding #3.

new_findings.title: Oracles don’t check if L2 sequencer is down for Chainlink feeds
new_findings.reason: Same L2 sequencer validation gap as finding #1.

new_findings.title: Chainlink Oracle does not check sequencer uptime
new_findings.reason: Same L2 sequencer validation gap as finding #1.

new_findings.title: Several functions in `seer.sol` may return erroneous data from Chainlink Oracle because it does not check if Sequencer is active
new_findings.reason: Same L2 sequencer validation gap as finding #1.

new_findings.title: Wrong oracle price used in case L2 sequencer uptime feed is down or restarted in less than grace period.
new_findings.reason: Grace period handling for L2 sequencer restart could be missing from PriceManager.

new_findings.title: Missing checks for whether the L2 Sequencer is active
new_findings.reason: Same L2 sequencer validation gap as finding #1.

new_findings.title: Seer.sol oracle will potentially return a wrong price if the Chainlink aggregator returns a price outside min/max range
new_findings.reason: PriceManager may not validate Chainlink aggregator min/max price bounds causing incorrect pricing.

new_findings.title: Use of chainlink's latest answer
new_findings.reason: Same deprecated API usage risk as finding #3.

new_findings.title: Use of depreciated chainlink API
new_findings.reason: Same deprecated API usage risk as finding #3.

new_findings.title: No checking for status of L2 sequencer on whether it is active or not
new_findings.reason: Same L2 sequencer validation gap as finding #1.

new_findings.title: Missing check for active Arbitrum Sequencer
new_findings.reason: Same L2 sequencer validation gap as finding #1.

new_findings.title: Using deprecated Chainlink function latestAnswer()
new_findings.reason: Same deprecated API usage risk as finding #3.

new_findings.title: _getPriceFeedPricе() in Oracle.sol missing checking sequencer status on L2
new_findings.reason: Same L2 sequencer validation gap as finding #1.

new_findings.title: Unchecked return price > 0 oracle
new_findings.reason: PriceManager._getAssetPrice() uses SafeCast int256→uint256 but may not validate price > 0 after casting.

new_findings.title: Zero Oracle price reading is allowed so is zero token sell price and zero swap min return
new_findings.reason: Same as finding 1 - zero price validation may be missing after SafeCast conversion.

new_findings.title: CHAINLINK'S MULTISIGS CAN IMMEDIATELY BLOCK ACCESS TO PRICE FEEDS AT WILL.
new_findings.reason: Chainlink V2 relies on Chainlink feeds which are inherently subject to multisig control and access restrictions.

new_findings.title: Upgraded Q -> 2 from #540 [1728827786267]
new_findings.reason: PriceManager likely doesn't check Chainlink's minAnswer/maxAnswer circuit breakers which could return incorrect bounded prices.

new_findings.title: Upgraded Q -> 2 from #532 [1727788244108]
new_findings.reason: Same min/max circuit breaker issue as finding 6.

new_findings.title: Upgraded Q -> 2 from #536 [1727787075969]
new_findings.reason: Same min/max circuit breaker issue as finding 6.

new_findings.title: Upgraded Q -> 2 from #537 [1727786736874]
new_findings.reason: Same min/max circuit breaker issue as finding 6.

new_findings.title: Upgraded Q -> 2 from #542 [1727784100266]
new_findings.reason: Same min/max circuit breaker issue as finding 6.

new_findings.title: [M-01] ChainlinkOracle::_fetchAndValidate will use incorrect price when underlying aggregator reaches minAnswer or maxAnswer
new_findings.reason: Core issue about minAnswer/maxAnswer bounds that would affect PriceManager's Chainlink integration.

new_findings.title: ChainlinkOracle.sol#spot() function will return wrong price when prices drop to minAnswer
new_findings.reason: Same minAnswer/maxAnswer circuit breaker vulnerability as other Loopfi findings.

new_findings.title: `AuraVault::_chainlinkSpot` has integration issues where it might return stale or incorrect results
new_findings.reason: PriceManager may not fully validate all return values from latestRoundData calls.

new_findings.title: Chainlink’s latestRoundData might return stale or wrong results
new_findings.reason: General latestRoundData validation issues could affect PriceManager's oracle calls.

new_findings.title: No check for active Arbitrum Sequencer in Chainlink Oracle
new_findings.reason: L2 Arbitrum sequencer uptime checks may be missing from oracle validation.

new_findings.title: latestRoundData() has no check for round completeness
new_findings.reason: Round completeness validation may be missing from Chainlink oracle calls.

new_findings.title: No Circuit-Breaker checks in `ChainlinkOracle.sol`
new_findings.reason: PriceManager lacks min/max circuit breaker validation despite having staleness checks.

new_findings.title: `AuraVault` reward calculation can be incorrect due to insufficient Chainlink `latestRoundData()` check
new_findings.reason: Round completeness validation not mentioned in PriceManager's staleness+fallback logic.

new_findings.title: ChainlinkOracle and PendleLPOracle does not handle minAnswer/maxAnswer circuit breakers
new_findings.reason: Same as #1 - min/max circuit breaker checks missing from PriceManager.

new_findings.title: ChainlinkOracleConnector assumes that all Chainlink feeds have a heartbeat of 24 hours
new_findings.reason: Dual oracle configuration may hardcode heartbeat assumptions per feed.

new_findings.title: Lack of Circuit Breakers in ChainlinkOracleConnector can be Exploited by Bad Actors
new_findings.reason: Same pattern as #1 and #3 - circuit breaker validation missing.

new_findings.title: Chainlink oracle will revert for price feeds with a wider heartbeat timeframe
new_findings.reason: Hardcoded heartbeat timeframes could cause reverts for some feeds.

new_findings.title: Chainlink oracle will return the wrong price if the aggregator hits `minAnswer`
new_findings.reason: Same min/max circuit breaker issue as previous findings.

new_findings.title: latestRoundData() has no check for round completeness
new_findings.reason: Round completeness validation not explicitly mentioned in PriceManager defenses.

new_findings.title: PriceFeed doesn't check min/max price boundaries
new_findings.reason: Min/max price boundary checks missing from PriceManager implementation.

new_findings.title: PriceOracle has invalid checks on price staleness.
new_findings.reason: Invalid staleness logic could exist despite dual oracle setup.

new_findings.title: PriceOracle missing checks for whether L2 Sequencer is active
new_findings.reason: L2 sequencer uptime checks not mentioned in PriceManager defenses.

new_findings.title: No check if Arbitrum/Optimism L2 sequencer is down in Chainlink feeds `PriceOracle.sol`
new_findings.reason: Same L2 sequencer validation gap as #17.

new_findings.title: Lack of message ordering may lead to failed transactions
new_findings.reason: Message ordering issues could affect CCIP bridge operations in FeeAggregator.

new_findings.title: The deposit may fail if the recipient is a contract account.
new_findings.reason: Contract recipient failures could affect auctionCallback() execution in AuctionBidder.

new_findings.title: The deposit may fail if the recipient is a contract account.
new_findings.reason: Duplicate of finding 12, same contract recipient issue applies.

new_findings.title: Lack of refund mechanism when extracting ZETA
new_findings.reason: Lack of refund mechanisms could apply to failed auction scenarios.

new_findings.title: Centralized dpx-eth oracle poses a big risk to the functionality of the protocol
new_findings.reason: Centralized oracle control through PRICE_ADMIN_ROLE in PriceManager poses similar risks.

new_findings.title: evm_hooks ignores some important errors
new_findings.reason: auctionCallback _multiCall might ignore important call failures

new_findings.title: ThecosomataETH.addLiquidity can be subject to sandwich attack
new_findings.reason: Dutch auction bidding could be vulnerable to MEV/sandwich attacks

new_findings.title: Contracts will not working correctly after February 2106. Vesting will be locked forever if withdrawn after February 2106.
new_findings.reason: Timestamp overflow after 2106 could affect any contract using uint32 timestamps, but specific usage unknown.

new_findings.title: finalize() use malicious clearingQuote can steal token
new_findings.reason: Auction settlement through bid() could potentially be manipulated if settlement logic has vulnerabilities.

new_findings.title: USDC blacklisted accounts can DOS the bidding system in Shortfall
new_findings.reason: USDC blacklisted accounts calling bid() could cause transfer failures and DoS the auction.

new_findings.title: Lack of auction existence and payment token validations in Seaport’s callback safeTransferFrom leads to critical impacts
new_findings.reason: isValidSignature() callback validation at settlement time could have similar validation gaps as Seaport.

new_findings.title: PegOracle: Division before multiplication causing significant loss of precision
new_findings.reason: Decimal precision loss could still occur in PriceManager despite decimal normalization defenses.

new_findings.title: PegOracle does not work with oracles that have 18 decimals
new_findings.reason: 18-decimal oracle handling issues could persist despite normalization defenses.

new_findings.title: Incorrect handling of pricefeed.decimals()
new_findings.reason: Incorrect decimal handling patterns could exist in PriceManager's price calculations.

new_findings.title: Price calculating errors
new_findings.reason: Price calculation errors from decimal mishandling could occur despite defenses.

new_findings.title: Oracle Risk: division issue and price decimal conversion issue result in oracle price undervalue
new_findings.reason: Oracle price undervaluation from decimal conversion issues could still exist.

new_findings.title: All PegOracle math fails if decimals does not equal 8
new_findings.reason: Math failures with non-standard decimal oracles could occur despite normalization.

new_findings.title: `PegOracle:latestRoundData` will always return zero for the nowPrice if the decimals are 18.
new_findings.reason: Zero price returns from 18-decimal precision loss could still happen.

new_findings.title: PegOracle.sol always returns 0 for assets with 18 decimals
new_findings.reason: 18-decimal assets returning zero due to precision loss remains possible.

new_findings.title: H02: some special oracle returned value will let the latestRoundData() return 0 at the second positon.
new_findings.reason: Special oracle values causing zero returns could affect PriceManager calculations.

new_findings.title: Oracle data feeds are insufficiently validated
new_findings.reason: Insufficient oracle validation beyond staleness could exist in PriceManager.

new_findings.title: Direct Transfers are not handled correctly
new_findings.reason: Direct transfers could potentially disrupt auction balance calculations despite controlled flow.

new_findings.title: UniswapV3 tokens of certain pairs will be wrongly valued, leading to liquidations.
new_findings.reason: Oracle decimal mismatch in PriceManager dual oracle system despite normalization defenses.

new_findings.title: Discrepency in the Uniswap V3 position price calculation because of decimals
new_findings.reason: Price calculation decimal discrepancy potential in PriceManager oracle interactions.

new_findings.title: Decimals of the oracle is not checked
new_findings.reason: Oracle decimal assumptions in PriceManager could be vulnerable despite stated normalization.
